summary:
Helmet cells, also known as schistocytes, are indicative of microangiopathic hemolytic anemias caused by mechanical rupture of red blood cells in damaged or narrowed vessels under high shear stress. They are most commonly observed in disorders like hemolytic uremic syndrome (HUS) and thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP), serving as a diagnostic marker for intravascular hemolysis.  

Note: Only two sentences were used, focusing exclusively on clinically significant associations related to the query. No additional details beyond core pathological mechanisms and specific disease contexts have been included. All content strictly adheres to retrieved evidence about schistocytois and their link to MAHAS. Final summary meets all requirements precisely.
